title: Roasted Brussels Sprouts with Chestnut Crumble & Smoked Bacon
summary: The best way to cook sprouts? Roasted in a hot oven and served with crispy bacon and crunchy breadcrumbs. Get stuck in.

method.description: Preheat the oven to 200°C / 400°F. Halve the sprouts, finely chop the chestnuts, dice the bacon, and chop the fresh parsley.
Toss the sprouts with ½ the olive oil and season with salt and black pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 mins, until crispy and browned.
In a pan, cook the bacon until crispy. Drain on paper towels.
In the same pan, add the remaining olive oil, chestnuts and breadcrumbs. Cook until the breadcrumbs are golden brown. Stir in parsley and season with salt and pepper.
Toss the roasted sprouts with the chestnut crumble and crispy bacon. Serve hot and enjoy!

chefs.name: Laurent Dagenais
chefs.summary:
Born and raised in Montreal, Dagenais is a chef by training who worked in the restaurant industry for almost a decade before a snowboarding accident changed his trajectory. “A decade ago my career took a turn that I would have never predicted,” explained Dagenais. “Breaking my arm put my cooking life on hold and that's why, in the midst of the pandemic, I started making videos on the internet.”
 
It was these videos that changed his life, making him an instant social media phenomenon. Reaching over 4+ million followers across his channels, Dagenais' main goal is to share his love of food and inspire people to get in to the kitchen more often.
